title: deadcode analysis: ~168 non-test-seam functions unreachable from cmd/evolve — including whole dormant subsystems (bridge/channel Supervisor, core PlanJudge, deliverable constructor family, checkpoint appliers, sandbox bwrap) each carrying permanent apicover test tax
summary: golang.org/x/tools deadcode from the ./cmd/evolve entrypoint reports 186 unreachable funcs; ~168 after excluding legitimate test seams (Fake*, withHooks, ForTesting). Clusters: bridge/channel/supervisor.go (whole Supervisor: Ask/awaitReply/collectSpan...), core/phase_judge.go (PlanJudge — known unwired), deliverable NewReviewer*/NewVerifier* constructor variants, checkpoint ComposeChecked/ApplyToStateFile/RecordPhaseIntegrity, adapters/sandbox GenerateBwrapArgv/New/Exec, core WithMaxPhaseIterations/WithWorktreeProvisioner/ApplyDefectsAsCarryoverTodos, cyclecost.ParseEventsLog (superseded by sidecar-preferred S7), changedpkgs.FromGit, verdict helpers. Full list preserved in the scan session; regenerate with: ~/go/bin/deadcode ./cmd/evolve (run in go/).
root_cause: Features landed dormant or were superseded without a removal pass; the apicover gate then forces name-tests for exports nobody calls, compounding maintenance cost.
fix: Case-by-case triage sweep, batched by package: for each cluster decide WIRE (a tracked plan exists to activate it — e.g. PlanJudge if the advisor roadmap still wants it) or DELETE (with its tests). Deletion is the default absent a concrete activation plan (YAGNI); dormant-by-design code kept must gain a doc comment naming the activating campaign. Regenerate the deadcode list at cycle start (do NOT trust the counts above blindly — reachability may have changed).
acceptance: Regenerated deadcode list attached to the build report; every cluster dispositioned WIRE/DELETE/KEEP-with-named-campaign
Deleted exports remove their apicover tests in the same commit; repo-wide go build/test/vet green
Net deadcode count reduced ≥60% of the non-seam baseline (~168 → ≤67); no behavior change (suite green, no regression)
notes: [FRESHNESS 2026-07-23] Regenerate the report FIRST: recurrence/retrofile + carryforward clusters have since gone live; PlanJudge still dead.
